steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The hypothesis claims P. pruinosus can serve as a proxy for X. laevis—meaning measurements in one species must reliably predict measurements in the other. Without this predictive correlation, the proxy has no value.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice C states that P. pruinosus bioaccumulation is NOT predictive of X. laevis bioaccumulation in comparable environments—directly destroying the predictive link the proxy depends on. Choices A and B describe differences between species but don't address whether measurements correlate; choice D would actually strengthen the hypothesis. That matches choice C — C is correct.
